content: src/ui/ is an isolated sub-package: own package.json, tsconfig (with project references for app+node), vite.config.ts, deps. Root tsconfig.json excludes src/ui; root biome.json excludes src/ui (not just src/ui/dist). The boundary between server and UI is the HTTP wire, so the UI duplicates row types in src/ui/src/api/types.ts rather than importing src/db/schema.ts.

title: HashRouter not BrowserRouter for the warren UI
rationale: API routes /runs/:id, /agents/:name are registered on the same Bun.serve and would shadow SPA deep links on a hard reload (e.g., GET /runs/abc123 → JSON, not index.html). HashRouter uses /#/runs/abc123 which lives entirely on the client; the server only ever sees / and serves index.html. Considered routing API under /api/ but SPEC §8.1 fixes the surface as flat. Considered Accept-header content negotiation but it's brittle. HashRouter is the cheapest V1 fix.

description: Vite + React tsc -b build inside src/ui/ fails with duplicate type declarations from bun-types in parent node_modules. tsc's auto type discovery walks up to the warren root node_modules and pulls in bun-types whose globals (ImportMeta.hot, ImportMetaEnv, Worker) conflict with vite/client and DOM lib.
resolution: Set 'types': [] in src/ui/tsconfig.app.json (rely on /// <reference types='vite/client' /> in src/vite-env.d.ts for client types). Set 'types': ['node'] in tsconfig.node.json for vite.config.ts (Node API). Add @types/node to devDeps. Set skipLibCheck: true in both as a belt-and-suspenders for any other stray @types parent.

content: UI auth: bearer token (WARREN_API_TOKEN) lives in localStorage under key 'warren.apiToken'. API client adds 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' to every fetch + NDJSON stream; on 401 it clears the cached token and throws UnauthorizedError so AuthGate redirects to /login. Login page probes via /agents (always 200 for valid token) rather than /readyz (returns 503 when zero agents are registered, which would falsely reject a fresh install).

name: NDJSON event stream consumption
description: useEventStream(runId, follow) reads the streamRunEvents async iterator (chunked NDJSON over fetch). follow=true keeps the connection open and reconnects on transport error with exponential backoff capped at 30s; lastSeqRef carries the highest seen seq so reconnects send ?since=lastSeq+1 to skip events already in the buffer. follow=false replays history once and stops (used for terminal runs). UnauthorizedError aborts permanently; the AuthGate redirects on the next render.

name: flex-main-min-w-0
description: Layout's <main className='flex-1'> wrapper must include min-w-0 so flex items shrink below their content's intrinsic min-width. Without it, wide tables (Projects, Runs) push the pane past 100vw and the page scrolls horizontally — even though the Table primitive already wraps in a 'w-full overflow-auto' div. min-w-0 unlocks the inner overflow-auto so tables scroll within their card instead of expanding the layout. Same trap for any future flex container holding scrollable content.

name: event-driven-runs-invalidation
description: RunDetail watches stream.events for kinds in REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS={state_change, cancel.requested, reap.completed, reap_failed} and calls qc.invalidateQueries({queryKey: ['runs']}) (no exact, so both ['runs', id] and the list page's ['runs', filter] refresh). Eliminates the stale-badge-after-cancel UX where the event arrived in the stream but the GET /runs/:id wasn't re-issued. The 3-5s refetchInterval polling stays as a backstop.

content: Mutation feedback in RunDetail uses the same inline text-xs pattern as SteerForm (no toast library is wired). CancelStatus reads mutation.isError/isSuccess/data.{alreadyTerminal,burrowRun.state} and renders feedback below the button. Avoids pulling sonner/react-hot-toast for one button.

RunDetail's empty-push and +N-commits header badges (warren-f3bb) read the latest reap.completed event off stream.events — the runs row itself doesn't store commitsAhead. Use extractReapSummary(events) to walk events backward, find kind==='reap.completed', narrow the unknown payload to ReapCompletedPayload, and read branchPushed + commitsAhead. Both badges only fire when branchPushed===true: 'empty push' (destructive variant) when commitsAhead===0; '+N commit(s)' (succeeded variant) when commitsAhead>0; nothing when commitsAhead is null (couldn't compute).

RunDetail surfaces reap-opened PR (warren-f6af) two ways: a 'PR ↗' header link in the badge row next to the +N-commits badge, and a dedicated 'Pull Request' MetaCard with the full URL when r.prUrl is non-null. Both read directly from RunRow.prUrl (mirrored from runs.pr_url), not from the reap.completed event payload — the run query is invalidated on reap.completed via REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS so the header refreshes without polling.

NewRunPage exposes a free-text 'Branch / tag / SHA (optional)' Input between the Project select and Prompt textarea (warren-7589). Selected project's defaultBranch is the placeholder. Empty/whitespace input is omitted from the POST /runs body via spread (...(trimmedRef.length > 0 ? { ref: trimmedRef } : {})) so the server's optionalString doesn't see an empty string and refreshProject falls back to project.defaultBranch. No remote-branch enumeration — the seed deferred git ls-remote on form load.

src/ui/src/api/client.ts runsApi.create takes a CreateRunInput type (defined in api/types.ts) so callers can spread an optional ref alongside agent/project/prompt without inlining the shape at every call site (warren-7589).

Project detail page lives at hash route /#/projects/:id (src/ui/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx). Wired in App.tsx after the /projects list route; the projects table makes the ID column a Link so the list is the entry point.

title: ProjectDetail reuses the projects-list cache
rationale: There is no GET /projects/:id endpoint — the list endpoint is the only project-row source. ProjectDetail useQuery(['projects']).data?.projects.find(p => p.id === id) avoids a redundant HTTP route and shares the cache with the list page so navigation back-and-forth never refetches the row.

content: Warren config panel (ProjectDetail.tsx) renders three blocks per LoadedWarrenConfig wire envelope: TriggersBlock (cron entries as cards), DefaultsBlock (defaultRole/defaultBranch/defaultPrompt as dl), ErrorsBlock (per-file failures, hidden when errors.length === 0). null fields render an EmptyHint instead of being skipped — operators see what's absent.

src/ui/src/api/types.ts mirrors the warren-config wire envelope manually (WarrenConfigResponse, Trigger, CronTrigger, DefaultsConfig, WarrenConfigFileError, WarrenConfigFileErrorCode). Kept in sync by hand because src/ui/ is excluded from the root tsconfig (mx-1bd551) — the boundary is the HTTP wire, not a TS import.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) auto-fills its agent picker from the selected project's .warren/defaults.json defaultRole (warren-fd14): a useEffect watches projectsApi.warrenConfig and sets agent=defaultRole when the role matches a registered agent and the user hasn't touched the picker. The 'agentTouched' state latches on the first user-driven onChange and never clears, so manual selections survive subsequent project switches. An inline text-xs hint under the select shows 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' when auto-filled, or a destructive-color warning when defaultRole exists but isn't a registered agent.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) auto-fills its prompt textarea from the selected project's .warren/defaults.json defaultPrompt (warren-af38), mirroring the defaultRole agent-picker pattern (warren-fd14): a promptTouched flag latches off the first user keystroke so manual edits survive subsequent project switches; the inline 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' hint surfaces below the textarea while the value still equals the default. Both effects share the same warrenConfig query (queryKey ['projects', project, 'warren-config']).

TriggersBlock (ProjectDetail.tsx) consumes GET /projects/:id/triggers — TriggerSummary[] envelope adds lastFiredAt/nextFireAt/lastRunId/parseError on top of the parsed YAML fields and lives at its own react-query key ['projects', id, 'triggers'] separate from the warren-config query (which still feeds DefaultsBlock + ErrorsBlock). Run Now uses a single useMutation at block level keyed by mutation.variables===triggerId; onSuccess invalidates the triggers query + runs and navigates to /runs/:newRunId (mirrors NewRun's onSuccess pattern, mx-d87018).

src/ui/src/api/types.ts mirrors the /projects/:id/triggers wire envelope as TriggerSummary + TriggersResponse + RunTriggerResponse — kept in lockstep with src/triggers/summary.ts TriggerSummary (root tsconfig excludes src/ui, so the boundary is the HTTP wire). RunTriggerResponse reuses the existing RunRow + BurrowSummary types since POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run returns the same envelope as POST /runs.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx) header + empty-state copy frames built-in claude-code + sapling as the default, and the canopy library as opt-in custom-agent layer on top (set CANOPY_REPO_URL). Avoid copy like 'Canopy prompts tagged agent:true' or 'clone your canopy library' in the empty state — it implies users must adopt canopy to use warren. NewRun page (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) subtitle says 'Spawn an agent against a project repo inside a fresh sandbox' (not 'a canopy agent ... burrow sandbox') for the same reason (warren-e576).

NewRun.tsx surfaces Provider override + Model override as paired free-text Inputs in a 2-col grid above the Prompt textarea (warren-f8c0). Auto-fill mirrors the agent picker pattern: read agent.renderedJson.frontmatter.{provider,model} via the local readFrontmatter() narrowing helper; populate the input until the operator types (providerTouched/modelTouched flags); trim empties before spawn.mutate so the wire stays optional-clean.

Cost display in RunDetail uses formatCostUsd(): >=1 USD → 2 decimals ('1.45'), <1 USD → 3 decimals ('0.045') so sub-cent cache-only turns don't round to zero. Rendered as a header badge (variant=succeeded, font-mono text-xs) with a tooltip listing tokens via formatTokens (>=1M -> '1.2M', >=1k -> '1.2k'). Runs.tsx list view exposes an opt-in Cost column persisted in localStorage under 'warren.runsList.showCostColumn'.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx EventLine (warren-70af) detects pi-runtime sub-kinds (compaction_start/end, auto_retry_start/end, extension_error, queue_update) by peeking at payload.type when event.kind is 'state_change' or 'telemetry' — burrow's pi parser (src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts) collapses pi's wide event vocabulary into burrow's stable taxonomy and stores the original pi envelope type in payload.type. PI_SUBKIND_LABELS maps each to a friendlier display label; extension_error additionally tints rose-700 like stderr. Kind-direct match (event.kind === 'compaction_start' etc.) is also honored so a future burrow release that promotes any of these to first-class kinds Just Works without a UI rev. Unknown kinds fall through to generic rendering (mx-0db923).

NewRun.tsx provider/model auto-fill follows the same precedence the server applies (warren-618b): project default (.warren/defaults.json.defaultProvider / defaultModel) > agent frontmatter. providerFromProjectDefault / modelFromProjectDefault drive the 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' label; falling back to 'Defaulted from agent frontmatter' when no project default is set. Auto-fill stops as soon as the operator touches the field.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx, warren-f755) expanded-row renders a structured AgentDefinitionPanel — metadata grid (name/version/source/provider/model/tags), resolvedFrom breadcrumb, each sections.* as a native <details> (system open by default), and a 'View raw JSON' toggle for parity. The expanded TableCell uses 'max-w-0' plus 'whitespace-pre-wrap break-words' inside every <pre> so long unbroken section bodies wrap at the cell width instead of widening the table and triggering page-level horizontal scroll.

AgentRow (src/ui/src/api/types.ts) carries an optional 'source: "builtin" | "library"' field. The server decorates it in withAgentSource (src/server/handlers.ts:181) via readAgentSource(renderedJson). UI consumers (e.g. AgentDefinitionPanel) display it but treat it as optional since older deployments may not emit it.

EventLine (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx, warren-3ad4) renders each RunEvent as a single-line <details> summary with seq/HH:MM:SS/displayKind/stream + a derived one-line summary; click expands to a pretty-printed JSON <pre>. summarizeEvent() defensively probes payload shape per kind: state_change reads payload.type then walks message.content for tool_use names and message.usage.{input,output,cost.total} to emit 'type · message (toolCall: X) · usage 154/23.8k · $0.026'; reap.* uses ReapCompletedPayload (state/branchPushed/commitsAhead/prUrl/errors); toolcall_*/tool_execution_* reads name/exit_code; text reads payload.text or delta; unknown shapes fall through to {text|message|reason|type} then JSON.stringify. group-open:hidden on the summary text drops the truncated preview once expanded. flex+min-w-0+truncate handles one-line clipping in the events scrollbox.

Cost column on /runs (src/ui/src/pages/Runs.tsx, warren-a7ec) is on by default. Default uses showCost=true; only an explicit '0' in localStorage key warren.runsList.showCostColumn hides the column. Rationale: pi runs are the common runtime and hydrate costUsd via in-stream extraction (warren-17a4).

RunDetail (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx, warren-a7ec) renders a dedicated CostCard inside the meta grid that shows formatCostUsd(costUsd) as font-mono text-lg with a tokens-in/out/cache-r/cache-w breakdown via formatTokenBreakdown(run) inline (not hover-only). Null costUsd renders an em-dash; null token breakdown is omitted. The old header cost Badge is removed.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx PreviewCard (warren-c0b9) renders when run.previewState !== null: status badge mapped to badge variants (starting→queued amber, live→succeeded green, failed→failed rose, torn-down→cancelled gray), an 'Open ↗' link routed through buildPreviewLoginUrl (auth-exempt handshake — token in query, server 302s to run-<id>.<host>), and a 'Tear down' button (POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown with actor='ui'). Failure tail surfaces in a code block when state==='failed'.

src/ui/src/api/client.ts: buildPreviewLoginUrl(runId) reads the bearer from localStorage and builds /runs/<id>/preview/login?token=<bearer>. Used by RunDetail's PreviewCard to render an 'Open ↗' link — the server's resolvePreviewRedirect falls back to https://run-<id>.<previewHost>/ when ?redirect is omitted, so the UI never needs WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST locally. Returns null when no token cached.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS includes preview_launched, preview_evicted, preview_torn_down (warren-c0b9). Each flips one of preview_state/preview_port/preview_started_at on the run row, so observing the event in the live NDJSON stream invalidates the run query and refreshes the PreviewCard without waiting for the 3s/5s polling backstop.

title: Preview teardown button only shown when previewState is in PREVIEW_ACTIVE_STATES ('starting'|'live')
rationale: Tearing down a 'failed' row would 200 with status='already-failed' and confuse the operator (nothing changed). Hiding the button for 'failed' and 'torn-down' makes the affordance only appear when it actually does something. The teardown route remains callable via API for scripting.

content: src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx PreviewCard (warren-016d) calls previewApi.config() with staleTime/gcTime=Infinity. Deployment-wide config (mode + host) only changes via a warren restart, so per-page refetch would be wasted cycles.

PreviewCard (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx) renders a mode-aware canonical URL line via formatPreviewUrl(): path mode uses window.location.origin (or config.host when set) + /p/<id>/; subdomain mode uses https://run-<id>.<host>/. Mirrors server-side src/preview/launch.ts formatPreviewUrl().

Tailwind v4 @theme tokens cannot be flipped by an explicit attribute selector when wrapped in @media — to support both OS-following (prefers-color-scheme) and explicit user override (data-theme on <html>), keep light tokens in @theme and define dark tokens in two parallel raw-CSS selectors on :root: ':root[data-theme="dark"]' for the explicit case and '@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) :root:not([data-theme])' for the OS-default case. Same --color-* oklch values in both blocks; @theme block unmodified. See src/ui/src/index.css (warren-d340).

Theme override persistence (warren-d340) lives at localStorage key 'warren.theme' with values 'light'|'dark'|'system'; 'system' is encoded as absence of the key. A FOUC-guard <script> in src/ui/index.html reads it synchronously and sets document.documentElement.dataset.theme before React paints — invalid stored values are dropped silently. useTheme (src/ui/src/hooks/useTheme.ts) is the single writer of the attribute at runtime; the prefers-color-scheme media query listener is only attached while theme === 'system'.

Warren brand mark wiring: src/ui/src/components/WarrenLogo.tsx renders the burrow-network mark as inline SVG using currentColor (inherits sidebar text color across themes); src/ui/public/favicon.svg is a self-contained variant with explicit grayscale fills + prefers-color-scheme style block — favicons cannot rely on currentColor across browsers. Vite auto-copies src/ui/public/* to dist/ root (no vite.config change). Geometry mirrors branding/generate-logo.py: pointed-top hex radius 32 around viewBox center 50,50; active node at index 1 (top-right).

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx (pl-bb70 step 6 / warren-c845) renders a 'Seed' MetaCard adjacent to Burrow ID / Burrow Run when r.seedId !== null. Mirrors the Pull Request MetaCard's conditional render pattern; rendered as monospaced text only (no hyperlink yet) because warren has no seeds UI route — R-04 will land /#/seeds/:id and convert this into a clickable back-link without changing the data shape. src/ui/src/api/types.ts RunRow now carries seedId: string | null; CreateRunInput carries optional seedId?: string for completeness even though no UI form passes it yet.

agentsApi.list({projectId?}, signal?) (src/ui/src/api/client.ts, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) takes a filter object first then an optional AbortSignal; empty projectId is converted to no query param (server rejects '?projectId='). agentsApi.get gained the same filter slot. agentsApi.refreshProject(projectId) hits POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh.

classifyAgentSource (src/ui/src/lib/agent-source.ts, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) collapses AgentRow.source ('builtin' | 'library' | 'project:<id>' | undefined) into { tier, label, projectId }. Tier is the coarse classifier for badges; the full project id is preserved in projectId for tooltip/comparison.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) keys row open-state on agentRowKey = '${source}::${name}' because the projectId filter can surface a global row and a project-tier row with the same name; a name-only key would expand both at once.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) scopes the agent picker to the selected project via useQuery(['agents', { projectId: project }], () => agentsApi.list(project ? { projectId: project } : {})); empty project falls back to a global-only fetch. AgentSourceHint renders a tier badge under the picker and flags project-tier rows whose projectId differs from the selected project.

title: Warren UI direction: thread/Plot-centric, not run-centric
rationale: Aligns warren with the network topology described in jayminwest.com/blog/12-agentic-networks. Run-centric UIs reify the orchestrator-to-worker pattern by making the human a dispatcher looking down at workers. Plot-centric UIs render the human as a peer node alongside agents. Tie-breaker test for any UI decision: does this make the human look like an operator above the agents, or a peer beside them? If 'above', it is hierarchy creeping back in and should be reconsidered. Steering becomes the default mode, dispatch the special case.

content: PlanRun UI surface (warren-a87f, pl-a258 step 7) lives at /plan-runs, /plan-runs/new, /plan-runs/:id. List page (src/ui/src/pages/PlanRuns.tsx) per-row fetches its own /plan-runs/:id detail to derive pending/in-flight/merged/failed child counts ('Np / Ni / Nm / Nf') because the list endpoint is row-only. Detail page (src/ui/src/pages/PlanRunDetail.tsx) polls every 5s and stops on PLAN_RUN_TERMINAL_STATES. NewPlanRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx) reuses NewRun's defaults flow and disables every form field plus shows a 'Plan runs require .seeds/' banner when selectedProject.hasSeeds is false; planId is a free-text input, promptTemplate textarea defaults to 'work on sd {seed_id}'. Plot wiring is explicitly NOT surfaced here (warren-06dc owns Phase 2).

name: plan-run-state-badge
description: src/ui/src/components/PlanRunStateBadge.tsx exports PlanRunStateBadge (PlanRunState) and PlanRunChildStateBadge (PlanRunChildState) as siblings of the existing StateBadge for runs. Maps to the same Badge variants (queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled/secondary): child 'pending'->secondary, 'dispatched'+'pr_open'->queued, 'merged'->succeeded, 'skipped'->cancelled. Pattern: badges are state-enum-specific tiny modules rather than one overloaded StateBadge — keeps the type narrow and adding new state enums (preview, child, plan-run) cheap.

content: src/ui/src/api/client.ts streamPlanRunEvents and streamRunEvents share a private streamNdjsonEvents(basePath, opts) helper: both endpoints use the same server-side eventToNdjson serializer so the wire shape (RunEvent envelope, runId discriminator) matches. Route any new NDJSON tail endpoint through streamNdjsonEvents instead of copying the fetch+reader+JSON-parse loop.

NewPlanRun.tsx (src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx, warren-b636 / pl-7937 step 7) surfaces an optional 'Plot ID' text input between Plan ID and Prompt template, rendered only when the selected project has BOTH hasSeeds=true AND hasPlot=true. The empty-string default is dropped before POST; the server gate (project_lacks_plot) is the source of truth, the UI gate just hides the input when it would be guaranteed to reject.

PlanRunDetail.tsx (src/ui/src/pages/PlanRunDetail.tsx, warren-b636 / pl-7937 step 7) renders a 'Plot' MetaCard with a Link to /plots/:id when planRun.plotId !== null. The href is a placeholder — warren-d362 (Phase 3) owns the live Plot detail route, so the link is intentionally a dead-link in V1 for navigation continuity once that page lands.

src/ui/src/api/types.ts mirrors the server-side Plot wire shapes (PlotSummary, PlotStatus, PLOT_STATUSES, CreatePlotIntentPatch, CreatePlotInput, ListPlotsResponse) for the /plots + /plots/:id surface (warren-4879 / pl-9d6a step 4). Wire field names stay snake_case verbatim (intent_goal_preview, attachments_count, last_event_ts, last_event_actor, project_id) because the server hands the PlotSummary interface straight through JSON.stringify; the input-side CreatePlotInput is camelCase and the plotsApi.create serializer rewrites projectId/dispatcherHandle to snake_case at the boundary. Same manual-mirror pattern as mx-7f971c — src/ui is excluded from root tsconfig so the boundary is the HTTP wire.

plotsApi in src/ui/src/api/client.ts (warren-4879 / pl-9d6a step 4) exposes list({status?}) → GET /plots?status= returning ListPlotsResponse {plots: PlotSummary[]} and create({projectId, name?, intent?, dispatcherHandle?}) → POST /plots returning PlotSummary (201). The create() serializer rewrites the camelCase input to snake_case body fields (project_id, dispatcher_handle) at the HTTP boundary; ApiError surfaces code 'project_lacks_plot' when the project hasn't opted into Plots, code 'bad_request' for unknown status strings on list.

PlotsPage (src/ui/src/pages/Plots.tsx, warren-e3e6 / pl-9d6a step 5) is the /plots route — sortable table (default last_event_ts desc; clicking column toggles dir; switching key resets to desc for last_event_ts, asc for name/status), status filter chip group driving server-side ?status= (mx-0b5f9c), and a New-Plot dialog whose project picker filters to hasPlot=true rows from /projects. Two empty-state branches: zero hasPlot projects → 'No Plot-enabled projects yet — run `plot init` in a project clone and refresh.' (verbatim copy pinned by spec); any hasPlot projects but list returned 0 → generic copy. List polls every 5s via tanstack-query (mx-268674).

Layout sidebar Plots nav entry (warren-2f55, pl-9d6a step 6) is gated on someProject.hasPlot=true via a useQuery against projectsApi.list with queryKey:['projects'] and staleTime:5000 so the Plots page query (same key) dedupes the fetch. When the gate is false BASE_NAV_ITEMS order (Runs, Plans, Projects, Agents) renders byte-identically to pre-Plot UI; when true Plots is spliced between Plans and Projects yielding Runs, Plans, Plots, Projects, Agents. Icon: Network from lucide-react.

PlotDetailPage (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-bdbf / pl-9d6a step 13) is the /plots/:id route — three-panel layout (IntentPanel left, SubstratePanel right, ActivityFeed full-width below). Polls via tanstack-query refetchInterval:5000 + staleTime:5000 (mx-268674 pattern); no live event stream yet (deferred per SPEC §11.O.Plot.UI). IntentPanel keeps an IntentDraft + dirty flag; useEffect reconciles draft FROM the server only when !dirty so a 5s poll mid-edit never clobbers user typing. Mutation failure leaves draft+dirty intact (no rollback) — the draft-restore-on-failure contract. Inputs disable when status is done/archived (frozen); server also rejects with plot_intent_frozen 409 as defense in depth. non_goals/constraints/success_criteria are one-item-per-line textareas; splitLines() trims+drops blanks before POST. SubstratePanel groups attachments by role via groupAttachmentsByRole (alphabetical by role; empty role rendered as '(no role)'); each row owns its own detach mutation that writes resp.envelope into the [plot,id] query cache so the UI converges without the 5s refetch. AddAttachmentDialog has a kind picker over ATTACHMENT_TYPES with per-kind ref placeholders+hints; role optional. ActivityFeed runs clusterEvents() — chains of 3+ consecutive same-type same-actor events collapse into a FoldedCluster (click to expand); 1- and 2-long runs stay expanded. EventLine borrows RunDetail's <details>/<summary> shape (mx-b97599): stable 10rem actor slot left, then type, summary, relativeTime; expanded body pretty-prints event.data as JSON. summarizePlotEvent switches on PlotEvent type for a one-line preview (e.g. 'from → to' for status_changed). Question-answer affordance, status-transition buttons, and the Run-plan button are intentionally deferred — separate pl-9d6a steps (warren-3c3e/6336/5d94).

plotsApi in src/ui/src/api/client.ts (warren-bdbf / pl-9d6a step 13 extends warren-4879/step 4) gained five mutation methods on top of list+create: get(plotId) → GET /plots/:id → PlotEnvelope; editIntent(plotId, {goal?, non_goals?, constraints?, success_criteria?, dispatcherHandle?}) → POST /plots/:id/intent flat-shape body (NOT wrapped under intent: like create) → PlotEnvelope; changeStatus(plotId, {next, dispatcherHandle?}) → POST /plots/:id/status → {summary, event}; attach(plotId, {kind, ref, role?, dispatcherHandle?}) → POST /plots/:id/attachments → {envelope, attachment}; detach(plotId, ref, dispatcherHandle?) → DELETE /plots/:id/attachments/:ref → {envelope, removed_id} (sends body only when dispatcherHandle is set — DELETE bodies are spec-legal but optional); answerQuestion(plotId, {eventId, answer, dispatcherHandle?}) → POST /plots/:id/questions/:event_id/answer → {event}. All write methods serialize dispatcherHandle to snake_case dispatcher_handle at the wire boundary; eventId is the targeted question_posed event's  ISO timestamp (PlotEvent has no synthetic id, only ). Wire mirror types live alongside in src/ui/src/api/types.ts: PlotEnvelope, PlotIntent, PlotAttachment, PlotEvent (discriminated union but typed as Record<string,unknown> data for forward-compat with future PlotEventType additions), ATTACHMENT_TYPES const tuple + AttachmentType, plus per-handler input/response envelopes.

RunPlanButton (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-5d94 / pl-9d6a step 14) renders on each sd_plan attachment row in SubstratePanel — gated by isSdPlanAttachment(a), which today treats type='seeds_issue' AND ref starting with 'pl-' as an sd_plan (plot-cli v0.3 has no dedicated 'sd_plan' AttachmentType; update the predicate when it does). Click opens RunPlanDialog showing read-only plan ref + project + plot_id + resolved agent + default prompt template; confirm calls plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun → POST /plan-runs with {project, planId, agent, promptTemplate:'work on sd {seed_id}', plotId, providerOverride?, modelOverride?}. Agent resolves from warrenConfig.defaults.defaultRole validated against agentsApi.list({projectId}); provider/model auto-fill mirrors NewPlanRun's defaults flow (mx-4c064b). On success navigates to /plan-runs/:id; ApiError surfaces inline with code (project_lacks_seeds, project_lacks_plot).

plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun (src/ui/src/api/client.ts, warren-5d94 / pl-9d6a step 14) is a thin sugar wrapper over POST /plan-runs — identical wire surface and request shape to planRunsApi.create. Lives on plotsApi so PlotDetail's Run-plan button reads as a Plot-side action; the server-side stacked gate (mx-4b7ff8) still rejects with project_lacks_seeds when .seeds/ is missing and project_lacks_plot when plotId targets a project without .plot/.

AnswerCard (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-3c3e / pl-9d6a step 15) is the inline question-answer affordance in PlotDetail's ActivityFeed. ActivityFeed builds a question_id->question_answered map once via buildAnswerMap (joined on answer.data.question_id === question_posed.at, matching src/plots/question-answerer.ts), then EventLine renders an AnswerCard (textarea + Submit) below open question_posed events and an AnsweredQuestionRow (compact 'answered · actor · text') below closed ones. Submit POSTs via plotsApi.answerQuestion and optimistically splices the returned event into the cached ['plot', plotId] envelope (dedup-by-at) so the card disappears immediately without waiting for the 5s refetch; on failure the draft is preserved in state and an inline error surfaces (draft-restore-on-failure pattern shared with IntentPanel).

PlotStatusBadge (src/ui/src/components/PlotStatusBadge.tsx, warren-6336 / pl-9d6a step 16) mirrors StateBadge.tsx's shape — thin wrapper around <Badge variant={status}>. Five SPEC §6.5 statuses get dedicated variants in components/ui/badge.tsx (drafting=zinc, ready=amber, active=blue, done=emerald, archived=zinc/muted) alongside RunState variants — the cva union is a strict superset so the two enums don't collide. Use this badge wherever a PlotStatus is displayed; do not inline rounded-full borders.

StatusTransitionControl (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-6336 / pl-9d6a step 16) renders the PlotDetail header's status pill + button group for SPEC §6.5 transitions. NEXT_STATUSES matrix is mirrored from src/plots/status-changer.ts STATUS_TRANSITIONS — kept as a UI-side constant so buttons render before the server round-trip; server re-validates authoritatively and PlotIllegalStatusTransitionError surfaces inline. Optimistic flow uses tanstack-query onMutate to patch cached envelope.status, rolls back in onError via the snapshot context, and onSuccess splices the returned status_changed event into event_log (dedup by .at) so the activity feed updates without waiting for the 5s poll. Archived button uses outline variant to de-emphasize the terminal transition.

PlotMetaCardContent (src/ui/src/components/PlotMetaCardContent.tsx, warren-37fd / pl-9d6a step 17) is the shared inner-content component for the 'Plot' MetaCard on PlanRunDetail.tsx and RunDetail.tsx. Does a tanstack-query plotsApi.get(plotId) with retry-disabled on ApiError 404 (mx-c6c9a5 client surface), staleTime+refetchInterval 30s, and renders three states: (1) 404 → 'Plot no longer available' muted italic + plotId mono fallback, no link (covers plan_runs.plot_id / runs.plot_id pointing at an archived or deleted Plot — mx-757be9 placeholder is now retired); (2) loading/other-error → working Link to /plots/:id with bare plotId text so the user can still click through and let PlotDetail surface the real error; (3) success → Link with plot.name + PlotStatusBadge (mx-1cc959) + plotId underneath. Shares the ['plot', plotId] query cache key with PlotDetail (mx-a3798b) so the click-through is warm. RunDetail also gains its first Plot MetaCard (rendered when run.plotId !== null) via the same component.

Plot-centric default landing (warren-e59a) is two-gated: someProject.hasPlot AND GET /plots returns ≥1 Plot. Implemented via DefaultLanding.tsx at the index route — redirects to /plots only when both hold, otherwise to /runs so the CLAUDE.md standalone path is byte-identical. Sidebar reorder in Layout.tsx mirrors the same gate (Plots → Runs → Plan Runs → Projects → Agents only when true). The PlotDetail ActivityFeed unifies human and agent events using the mx-b97599 EventLine shape with the actor slot as the only visual distinction; 3+ same-kind/same-actor chains collapse. Polling cadence is 5s with tanstack-query staleTime: 5s (mx-268674), so reap-mirrored agent events converge with synchronous UI writes within one poll.

NewPlanRun.tsx missing-.seeds error card (warren-c666) renders an inline 'Refresh project' Button that calls projectsApi.refresh(project) and invalidates the projects query on success — same useMutation pattern as Projects.tsx, so a stale has_seeds flag (project registered pre-warren-9990) can be reconciled without leaving the page.

RunSeedButton (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-ff2a) renders on every seeds_issue attachment in SubstratePanel and navigates to /runs/new via react-router location.state ({project, agent, plotId, prompt}). Prompt template defaults to .warren/defaults.yaml defaultPrompt with {seed_id} substitution; falls back to 'work on sd {seed_id}'.

NewRunPage (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx, warren-ff2a) accepts NewRunRouteState via react-router location.state to pre-fill project/agent/plotId/prompt. Pre-filled non-empty agent/prompt mark *Touched=true so NewRun's project-default auto-fill effects don't clobber the values. Empty initial agent still lets the default auto-fill take over once warrenConfig + agents load.

BatchDispatchAllButton (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-7c3f / pl-5310 step 3) renders in SubstratePanel's header when the Plot has >=1 batch-eligible attachment (seeds_issue && !isSdPlanAttachment, since sd_plan rows already own RunPlanButton). Clicking opens BatchDispatchDialog which resolves agent/prompt from warrenConfig.defaults (same path as RunSeedButton mx-8677f3), then fires N parallel runsApi.create POSTs each carrying {plotId, seedId, prompt with {seed_id} substituted, optional provider/model overrides}. Per-target outcome rows flip pending -> dispatched (linked to /runs/:id) or failed (with ApiError code in title). V1 is parallel-only; serial-gated-on-PR-merge mode is deferred to pl-5310 step 4 (Plot->synthesized seeds plan->plan-run) to avoid building a stop-gap queue that step 4 immediately deprecates (pl-5310 risk #4). Closed-seed skip from warren-ea66 acceptance (d) is also deferred: requires a seed-status read path the UI doesn't have plumbed; operator spot-checks the visible target list in the confirm dialog instead. Server-side, each /runs POST with plotId emits a run_dispatched event onto the Plot event_log (SPEC 11.O), so the activity feed picks all N up within one 5s poll tick without any extra client work.

RefreshProjectsCTA (src/ui/src/components/RefreshProjectsCTA.tsx, warren-bb22) renders a small 'Refresh projects to discover new Plots' button that fans out projectsApi.refresh(id) across every registered project in parallel via Promise.all, then invalidates [projects], [plots], and [plot] react-query keys on success. Used in two places: (1) Plots.tsx EmptyState (both 'no hasPlot projects' and 'no plots yet' branches — but NOT the statusFiltered branch, since refresh wouldn't change the filter), (2) PlotDetail.tsx 404/isError branch, where it's the inline recovery path for a user who just committed a Plot but hasn't refreshed yet (mx-62ef33: detectProjectFeatures only flips hasPlot during refresh). Disabled when projects list is loading or empty. Optional 'label' prop overrides the default button text.

DispatchAsPlanRunButton (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-bce0 / pl-f404 step 4 / SPEC §11.Q) renders alongside BatchDispatchAllButton (warren-7c3f) in SubstratePanel's header, gated on the same isBatchDispatchTarget≥1 filter. Recommended path: synthesizes a seeds plan from the Plot's open seeds_issue attachments and dispatches via POST /plot-plan-runs (plotsApi.dispatchSynthesizedPlanRun, which maps camelCase to snake_case body), navigating to /plan-runs/:id on success. The confirm dialog renders the synthesized plan title preview ('Plot <id> synthesized plan-run', mirroring src/plot-plan-runs/synthesizer.ts), candidate seed list, and a filter-rationale subheader noting that closed seeds are dropped server-side at synthesis time and pl-* refs are excluded as sd_plan attachments. Resolves agent + provider/model overrides from .warren/defaults.yaml the same way RunPlanDialog does (mx-4c064b / mx-be04a6). The BatchDispatchDialog description was updated to point operators at the plan-run path as recommended and reframe itself as the parallel-fan-out escape hatch.

plotsApi.dispatchSynthesizedPlanRun (src/ui/src/api/client.ts, warren-bce0) is the typed POST /plot-plan-runs facade. Unlike planRunsApi.create (POST /plan-runs uses camelCase body keys), this handler reads snake_case (plot_id, project_id, agent_name, prompt_template, ref, provider_override, model_override, dispatcher_handle) — the client maps CreatePlotPlanRunInput's camelCase to the snake_case wire envelope at the call site. Response shape (CreatePlotPlanRunResponse) extends CreatePlanRunResponse's {planRun, children} with synthesis-specific {synthesizedPlanId, parentSeedId}, so consumers can navigate to /plan-runs/:id AND surface the synthesized plan id for debugging.

name: plotdetail-batch-dispatch-seed-status-probe
description: PlotDetail BatchDispatchDialog probes seed status via projectsApi.seedStatus on dialog open (one fetch per target, in parallel via Promise.all in the queryFn). Closed seeds are marked outcome.status='skipped' via an effect that re-seeds outcomes once the probe resolves; dispatchAll skips closed refs (closedRefs Set) so no POST /runs fires for them. Dialog header counts dispatchable seeds; skipped count surfaced inline. Failed probes degrade to status='unknown' (still dispatchable) rather than blocking the dialog.

name: Interactive Chat component primitive
description: src/ui/src/components/Chat.tsx (pl-0344 step 10 / warren-ea98) is the reusable streaming chat surface for interactive runs. It consumes useEventStream(runId, follow), filters RunEvents by kind ('user_message' / 'agent_message'), maps payload.{actor,content} into ChatMessage bubbles, and posts new turns via runsApi.sendMessage (POST /runs/:id/messages). onTurnSpawned(turnRunId) lets the parent (e.g. PlotDetail in warren-444c) re-anchor its event stream onto the freshly-spawned turn because the server appends user_message and (later) agent_message onto the new turn's run id, not the conversation handle. Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submits. No ScrollArea/Tabs install needed — the step said 'if needed' and a flex column with overflow-y-auto + auto-scroll on messages.length suffices.

content: PlotDetail InteractivePanel (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-444c / pl-0344 step 11) renders 'Start brainstorming' / 'Run planner' / 'Formalize' buttons over an inline <Chat/> bound to the latest interactive run on the Plot. The active-run anchor is found by walking event_log in reverse for 'run_dispatched' events whose data.agent is in INTERACTIVE_AGENT_NAMES (brainstorm/planner); Chat's onTurnSpawned re-anchors activeRunId onto each spawned turn so the parent doesn't follow the prior turn's id. Start/Run-planner open a DispatchInteractiveDialog that POSTs runsApi.createInteractive (mode=interactive, interactiveAgent=<name>, plotId, project); Formalize opens FormalizeDialog which GETs plotsApi.formalize once (staleTime: Infinity, gcTime: 0) and applies the edited suggestion via plotsApi.editIntent on accept. Whole panel is disabled when status is done/archived (frozen).

runsApi.createInteractive (src/ui/src/api/client.ts, warren-444c) is the typed POST /runs wrapper for spawning the first interactive turn bound to a Plot. Sends both agent and interactiveAgent under the same name (the server still requires the plain 'agent' field even when 'interactiveAgent' overrides it — see parseRunMode + createRunHandler in src/server/handlers.ts) plus mode='interactive' and plotId. plotsApi.startBrainstorm wraps POST /brainstorm for the atomic create-Plot+first-turn fresh-start path (snake_case body); plotsApi.formalize wraps POST /plots/:id/formalize and is non-mutating (no Plot event, no run spawned).

PlotEnvelope.paused_runs (src/ui/src/api/types.ts, src/plots/reader.ts, warren-4ea4 / pl-0344 step 12) is a server-resolved snapshot of warren `runs` rows in state=paused bound to the Plot. Each PausedRunInfo carries {run_id, paused_at, paused_question_event_id, pause_timeout_ms}. PlotDetail's ActivityFeed builds a pausedByQuestion Map keyed on paused_question_event_id (joined to question_posed.at, same id contract as AnswerCard's :event_id) and forwards it through FoldedCluster → EventLine → AnswerCard. When a question has a matching paused row, EventLine renders <StateBadge state="paused" /> inline on the summary, and AnswerCard promotes to a primary-bordered 'Answer & resume' affordance with a 1s-tick PauseCountdown anchored on paused_at + pause_timeout_ms (flips to a destructive 'budget elapsed — agent will resume with timeout warning' past zero; submit stays enabled either way). Resolved server-side in src/server/handlers.ts loadPausedRunsForPlot() via repos.runs.listByPlotId(plotId) filter→paused, with pauseTimeoutMs sourced from WarrenConfigCache → loadWarrenConfig → DEFAULT_AGENT_PAUSE_TIMEOUT_MS fallback (mirrors src/runs/pause.ts resolveBudget()). StateBadge gains a 'paused' RunState variant (amber/yellow) wired into the badge.tsx cva.

PlotsPage 'Needs you' chip + Start brainstorming dispatcher (src/ui/src/pages/Plots.tsx, warren-f0e2 / pl-0344 step 13). The 'Needs you' toggle sets React state needsAttention which adds filter='needs_attention' to plotsApi.list; statusFilter stays orthogonal (server composes ?filter + ?status per the listPlotsHandler contract). StartBrainstormDialog calls plotsApi.startBrainstorm({projectId,prompt}) (POST /brainstorm — atomic Plot+run create) and navigates to /plots/:id on success so inline Chat picks up the streamed first turn (mx-0ea49c). Row-level needs-you badges read PlotSummary.reasons — server only populates that field when filter=needs_attention, so unfiltered lists render no badges. The query key includes the needs-attention flag so toggling refetches against the new params instead of serving the stale cached list.

Layout sidebar needs-attention badge + DefaultLanding Plot-first redirect (warren-f0e2 / pl-0344 step 13). Layout.tsx polls GET /plots/needs-attention/count every 10s via plotsApi.needsAttentionCount, gated on anyHasPlot so standalone deployments pay zero. NavItem gained optional badge: number rendered as a small primary-tinted pill (capped '99+'); only the Plots entry shows it today. DefaultLanding.tsx now redirects to /plots **unconditionally** whenever anyHasPlot=true even with zero Plots — the empty Plots page itself surfaces 'Start brainstorming' / 'New Plot' affordances so an empty list is a first-class state. Removes the previous second useQuery on ['plots','all']. PlotSummary in src/ui/src/api/types.ts gained an optional reasons?: NeedsAttentionReason[] field mirroring the server-side PlotNeedsAttentionSummary; NEEDS_ATTENTION_REASONS is re-exported there in canonical paused_run/merged_pr_unreviewed/stale_draft order.

src/ui/src/pages/PlotSummary.tsx (warren-8917 / pl-0344 step 15) is the institutional-memory artifact view at /plots/:id/summary, registered in App.tsx after /plots/:id. It fetches via plotsApi.summary() (GET /plots/:id/summary returning PlotSummaryArtifact) with staleTime+refetchInterval at 30s (less aggressive than PlotDetail's 5s — this view is a reference, not a live workbench). Layout is a stack of Card sections: Header (name + PlotStatusBadge + created/last/done dates + back link), Intent (goal + non_goals/constraints/success_criteria via StringListBlock), Decisions, Linked PRs (with merged_at badge), Linked commits, Linked seeds, Timeline (structural events from PlotSummaryTimelineEntry, kind-filtered server-side). PlotDetail.tsx links to it via 'view summary' inline next to the project-id link in the header.

name: mobile-nav-drawer
description: Mobile nav drawer uses Radix Dialog primitives directly (not the wrapped DialogContent) to anchor a left-side slide-over panel via fixed inset-y-0 left-0; whole subtree gated by md:hidden so desktop never instantiates portal nodes. useEffect on location.pathname auto-closes the drawer on route change so NavLink clicks dismiss it cleanly under HashRouter.

content: On responsive table pages (warren-0155, pl-4ed6 step 2: Runs.tsx, PlanRuns.tsx, Projects.tsx), the table-cell strategy on narrow viewports is whitespace-nowrap on every TableHead + TableCell so the columns retain their natural width and the existing overflow-auto wrapper in components/ui/table.tsx provides clean horizontal scroll — instead of letting cells wrap and produce a squished, multi-line table. Card header rows that pair a title with a secondary stat (e.g. Runs.tsx CardTitle + cost total) get flex-wrap + gap-2 so they stack on small screens. Filter rows with an ml-auto pushed control (e.g. PlanRuns.tsx project <select>) should switch to w-full sm:ml-auto sm:w-auto so the control becomes a full-width row on mobile rather than awkwardly floating after a flex-wrap break.

Runs page (src/ui/src/pages/Runs.tsx, warren-ee50 / pl-b0c0 step 1) paginates via offset+pageSize state (PAGE_SIZE_OPTIONS=[25,50,100,200], default 50; persisted under warren.runsList.pageSize). Page footer renders rows-per-page <select> + prev/next ChevronLeft/Right buttons + 'X\u2013Y of T' counter. Offset is intentionally NOT persisted (a stale offset can land past the end of a shrunken result set; reset-on-reload is the conservative default); offset DOES reset to 0 on any change to filter / sort / dir / pageSize via a useEffect with the offset-setter as side-effect (biome-ignore lint/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies). totalRuns + costTotals come from the server envelope (data?.total / data?.costTotalUsd / data?.costPricedCount) so the card title and cost summary are all-time, not visible-page.

name: mobile-responsive-table-pages
description: Mobile-responsive table pages follow a consistent recipe: wrap raw <table> in 'relative w-full overflow-auto' (or use the Table component which provides this), apply 'whitespace-nowrap' to every th/td except wide free-text columns (which get 'max-w-[16rem] truncate'), and on CardHeaders pairing a title with action buttons use 'flex flex-row flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2 space-y-0' so buttons drop to a second row on narrow viewports. For PlotDetail's activity-feed timestamp/actor column (ActorSlot w-40 + ml-[11rem] indent), use 'w-24 sm:w-40' and 'ml-28 sm:ml-[11rem]' so the indent stays proportional on mobile while preserving the desktop two-column scan.

name: mobile-form-touch-targets
description: Form pages bump inputs/selects/buttons to h-11 sm:h-9 and text-base sm:text-sm on mobile: 44px touch targets and 16px font size to suppress iOS Safari auto-zoom on focus, while desktop sm+ keeps the original h-9/text-sm compact sizing. Footer Cancel/Dispatch button row uses flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:justify-end gap-2 with each button w-full sm:w-auto so primary action lands at the bottom of the stack on mobile (thumb reach) and right-aligned on desktop.

content: InteractivePanel (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-537e / pl-b0c0 step 2) is gated behind an experimental Badge + amber notice banner because appendAgentMessage (src/runs/interactive.ts) has no production call site — reap never appends the agent_message event, so the agent's reply lands on the run's event log instead of the Chat transcript. Dispatch + Formalize + question-answer still work end-to-end; only the live agent-reply render is broken. To lift: wire reap to extract claude-code's final assistant turn from the bridge stream and call appendAgentMessage, then drop both the Badge and the amber banner in InteractivePanel + the mirrored empty-state caveat copy. Tracked as follow-up warren-509f.

PlotNameEditor (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-bed0 / pl-b0c0 step 3) is the inline header rename affordance. Default state shows

{plot.name}

+ a ghost 'rename' button; clicking swaps in an Input + Save/Cancel buttons with Enter-submit / Escape-cancel keybinds and autoFocus. Short-circuits empty-after-trim and no-change submissions client-side so the user doesn't pay a round-trip for a no-op. plotsApi.rename(plot.id, {name}) returns the full PlotEnvelope which replaces the ['plot', plot.id] cache entry directly via qc.setQueryData, then ['plots'] is invalidated so the list view re-fetches the new name. Errors surface inline (ApiError → 'message (code)', other → String(error)) without dropping out of edit mode. useEffect re-syncs draft from plot.name when NOT editing so a concurrent rename from elsewhere doesn't clobber the user's in-flight edit.

StatusTransitionControl (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-470e / pl-b0c0 step 4) renders as a native <select> dropdown + Apply button, replacing the prior per-next-status Button group. Dropdown lists every PlotStatus other than plot.status from PLOT_STATUSES; entries not in NEXT_STATUSES[plot.status] render disabled with a '(not allowed from <current>)' hint so operators see the full SPEC §6.5 matrix and understand reachability rather than guessing why a phase is missing. Selection state is local useState<PlotStatus|''>; a useEffect resets it to '' whenever plot.status changes (own mutation or 5s refetch picking up out-of-band change). canApply guards against pending + illegal targets; Apply button text flips to '→ <next>…' while mutation.isPending. Optimistic cache patching + onError rollback contract from warren-6336 is preserved unchanged; server contract (POST /plots/:id/status) is identical so no API/test churn. Archive still uses outline variant for visual de-emphasis.

PlanRuns.tsx (warren-2235 / pl-b0c0 step 5) shows per-plan cost in a Cost column computed client-side by summarizeCost() over detail.data.runs from each row's planRunsApi.get poll — no API change, the detail endpoint already fans child runs out via runs.listByIds. NULL-aware (mirrors RunsRepo.aggregate, mx-2ae43b): only non-null costUsd is summed, priced count surfaces ghost runs whose cost was never recorded via a tooltip on the cell. PlanRunDetail.tsx gets a parallel Cost MetaCard with the same posture (summarizeRunCost). formatCostUsd is re-imported from RunDetail.tsx — same sub-dollar 3-decimal / ≥ 2-decimal shape as the Runs page header (Runs.tsx line 191) and run badges so the three cost surfaces read identically.

CostAnalyticsPage (src/ui/src/pages/CostAnalytics.tsx, warren-cf63 / pl-b0c0 step 6) is the /cost-analytics route, registered in App.tsx and surfaced as a 'Cost' sidebar entry in Layout.tsx (DollarSign icon, after Agents — operator analytics view, not a daily-driver page). Single-shot fetch via analyticsApi.cost({projectId?,from?,to?}); filters persist in URL query (useSearchParams over HashRouter). Renders a totals card + 8 BreakdownCard panels in a 1/2-col responsive grid (date, project, plan, plot, agent, model, provider, run). Bucket key=COST_ANALYTICS_NONE_KEY renders as em-dash; project/plot/run/agent keys deep-link to their detail pages, plan keys are plain text (no plan detail UI). Long-tail rows are truncated at 30 (50 for 'run') with a '+N more' footer.

PlotSyncButton (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, warren-1d0c) renders a manual 'Sync to GitHub' button in the header under StatusTransitionControl that triggers plotsApi.sync(plotId) and surfaces statusMessage (success, info, error) with optional prUrl anchor link.
Import RefreshProjectsCTA from @/components/RefreshProjectsCTA.tsx and pass a label prop to customize button text

RunPlanDialog in src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx (warren-6e4c / pl-f666 step 1) ships an editable promptTemplate Textarea instead of the previous ReadOnlyField. Pattern mirrors NewPlanRun.tsx ~line 300-320: controlled [promptTemplate, setPromptTemplate]=useState(DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE) + [promptTouched, setPromptTouched]=useState(false), trimmedPrompt feeds plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun({promptTemplate}), Dispatch button disabled when trimmedPrompt.length===0, and the 'Default.' hint vanishes once touched. State resets on close-and-reopen because RunPlanButton conditionally mounts/unmounts RunPlanDialog (no useEffect reset needed). DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE module constant ('work on sd {seed_id}') unchanged; no API/prop signature changes.

name: ui-source-structural-test
description: Warren's UI package (src/ui) ships without a React test harness (no jsdom/@testing-library). For UI invariants that need regression pinning, write a structural source-level test that readFileSyncs the .tsx and asserts on substring/regex patterns inside a function body extracted via brace-balance walk. Place at src/ui-tests/<page>.test.ts (outside src/ui so it's picked up by root tsconfig + root bun test discovery, and not by src/ui's own tsconfig/build). First instance: src/ui-tests/plot-detail-run-plan-dialog.test.ts (warren-fdf0 / pl-f666 step 2) pins RunPlanDialog's editable-prompt invariants (useState(DEFAULT_PROMPT_TEMPLATE), Textarea bound to promptTemplate, trimmedPrompt flowing into plotsApi.dispatchPlanRun, readyToDispatch disabling Dispatch on empty, conditional-mount reset, unchanged prop signatures). biome.json already excludes src/ui from lint; src/ui-tests is included in lint and root typecheck so the test is a typed, formatter-checked artifact. Use this pattern when introducing 'no React infra' UI tests instead of pulling in jsdom.

content: Phase 1 design-token foundation (warren-23fe / pl-55a3 step 1) lives in src/ui/src/index.css and locks the source-of-truth dark seam. (1) Brand fonts self-hosted via @fontsource-variable/inter + @fontsource-variable/jetbrains-mono — DO NOT import the package's wght.css / index.css; those ship per-subset @font-face for cyrillic/greek/vietnamese/latin-ext/latin and inflate CSS past the bundle-size ratchet (+3.3KB raw / +3.3KB gzip in practice). Instead declare 2 inline @font-face rules pointing at <pkg>/files/<face>-latin-wght-normal.woff2 — Vite resolves the package URL and emits hashed copies in dist/assets/. Variable fonts cover the full 100-900 weight axis from one woff2 per family. (2) @custom-variant dark (&:where([data-theme="dark"], [data-theme="dark"] *)) supersedes mx-7fc92a: the dark token block is now a single :root[data-theme="dark"] selector (no @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) duplicate) because useTheme + the FOUC script in src/ui/index.html now resolve theme==='system' to a concrete data-theme=light|dark on <html>. This means Tailwind's dark: utilities now track the user toggle, not OS preference. (3) Token surface declared in @theme: cool-neutral hue 264 for the gray ramp (light + dark), muted-green --color-primary (hue 152, chroma ~0.10) for brand, semantic status palette (--color-success/warning/info/danger/neutral) without -foreground pairs (Phase 4 Alert/Toast will add them when consumed), --radius-sm/md/lg/xl/2xl, tinted --shadow-sm/md/lg (oklch 264 hue), --z-sticky/dropdown/overlay/modal/toast. tabular-nums applied via global selector on table/code/pre/.font-mono/[class*=tabular].

Phase 2a primitive upgrades (warren-6e69 / pl-55a3 step 2) ship minimal-but-real upgrades scoped by the bundle-size ratchet: Button picks up active:opacity-80 on all variants (default/destructive get the deeper press, outline/ghost reuse the same rule for chrome variants); Card grows a CVA with default + elevated variants only (interactive/flat were dropped to fit budget — Phase 6/warren-e6b3 will add them when callers actually need them); Dialog adds active:opacity-90 + duration to the close button. Input/Textarea/Badge/Table held flat: token-driven hover for those primitives + a Badge status refactor onto the Phase 1 --color-success/warning/info/danger/neutral tokens would have blown the CSS raw ceiling, and Badge is folded into Phase 3 (warren-3849)'s StatusIndicator registry anyway. CSS+JS raw budgets bumped 350B each (35200→35550, 475460→475810) with rationale in scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json — this is the second and final raise the design-system phases get; Phase 2b onward must hold flat or shrink. Gzip ceilings unchanged (the new utilities compress well against existing ones).

src/ui/src/api/client.ts exports runAnalyticsApi.runs(filter, signal) hitting GET /analytics/runs, mirroring analyticsApi.cost exactly (URLSearchParams for projectId/from/to). Response types (RunAnalyticsResponse + totals/timeSeries/byAgent|Model|Provider/byFailureReason/topSeedsByContext) mirror buildRunMetrics in src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts; RUN_ANALYTICS_NONE_KEY='__none__' matches the server NONE_KEY sentinel (warren-df6e / pl-ad0f step 4).

name: RunAnalytics page Phase 1 + recharts code-split
description: RunAnalytics page Phase 1 (warren-638a / pl-ad0f step 5) lives at hash route /#/run-analytics (src/ui/src/pages/RunAnalytics.tsx) with sub-components under src/ui/src/pages/run-analytics/ (KpiCards, Charts, Tables, format.ts) to stay under the 500-line file budget. Mirrors CostAnalytics: useSearchParams filter (projectId/from/to) over HashRouter, single GET /analytics/runs round-trip via runAnalyticsApi.runs, defaults to last 30 days. recharts is code-split: App.tsx lazy-loads BOTH analytics routes (React.lazy + Suspense) so recharts lands in its own RunAnalytics chunk (~120KB gzip) and the initial index chunk holds ~167KB gzip, honoring the warren-876c directive (totals.gzip.js nudged +400B; JS ratchet suspended until warren-9d89). recharts Tooltip formatter must type its value param as 'number | string | readonly (number|string)[] | undefined' to satisfy recharts 3.x Formatter<ValueType,NameType> under tsc -b (standalone tsc --noEmit misses it). Sidebar nav label is 'Run stats' (BarChart3 icon) to avoid colliding with the existing 'Runs' (Activity) item.

content: InteractivePanel agent-reply limitation LIFTED (warren-509f): reap now wires appendAgentMessage via src/runs/reap/interactive.ts. captureInteractiveReply({run, input, now}) runs as reap's last step (after every emit()/fail() so the seq allocator and appendAgentMessage's MAX(seq)+1 lookup can't collide). extractFinalAssistantMessage scans persisted event rows and concatenates the trailing assistant turn = all stdout kind=text events after the last tool_use/tool_result (payload.text, content fallback; thinking blocks ignored), returns null when no assistant text. It appends an agent_message event and publishes it to the broker so a still-following Chat lights up live; best-effort (errors logged, never block reap). The experimental Badge + amber banner + empty-state caveat copy were dropped from src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx (Badge import removed). Note: src/runs/reap/run.ts sits exactly at the 500-line file-size budget after this wiring. Supersedes mx-545b22.

LeveretPage (src/ui/src/pages/Leveret.tsx, warren-763f) is the /leveret top-level overseer home — a conversations list backed by GET /conversations via conversationsApi.list({project?,plot?,status?}) in client.ts; status filter chips drive the server ?status query, rows link to /leveret/:id (split-view stubbed by warren-01c8) and to /plots/:id. Leveret + Plots nav entries are both gated on anyHasPlot in Layout.tsx; plot-enabled order is Leveret → Plots → Runs → Plans → Projects → Agents, byte-identical BASE_NAV_ITEMS otherwise.

ConversationDetail page (src/ui/src/pages/ConversationDetail.tsx, warren-01c8) is the /leveret/:id Leveret split-view: LEFT reuses Chat with a new sendMessage override prop so operator turns ride POST /conversations/:id/messages (persist+steer the long-lived mode:conversation anchoring run) instead of spawning a fresh turn; RIGHT renders the Plot intent DYNAMICALLY via Object.entries(intent) (no hardcoded goal/non_goals fields, §0.0.A), editable via plotsApi.editIntent and live-updating via a 4s refetchInterval on the plot query. Top-bar 'Send to planner' is gated on intentIsNonEmpty(baseFields); actual send-off wiring lands in warren-756d/6e45. conversationsApi gained get()+postMessage().

Because src/ui is excluded from the root tsconfig, the frontend API layer types must be duplicated from the server-side counterparts. Ensure CreateConversationInput, CreateConversationResponse, SendOffConversationResponse, and RewakeConversationResponse remain aligned.

title: Leveret New Conversation Dialog
rationale: Allows operators to start a Leveret conversation directly from the Overseer page, picking between auto-creating a Plot or attaching an existing one, and entering an opening prompt.

title: Extract planner send-off flow into sub-component
rationale: Extracted the destructive and complex send-off action flow into a modular SendOffButton component under pages/conversation-detail/ to keep the parent ConversationDetail.tsx far below its 500-line budget and improve testability.

name: chat pi vocabulary rendering
description: chat-messages.ts toMessage() (warren-4ccc) materializes BOTH the legacy brainstorm vocabulary (user_message/agent_message, payload.content) AND the pi-chat/Leveret vocabulary into ChatMessage bubbles: text->agent (payload.text), thinking->collapsible 'thinking' kind (payload.text), tool_use->compact 'tool' one-liner (payload.name + first string arg of payload.arguments), tool_result->'tool' one-liner (payload.toolName + content[].text snippet, payload.isError flag). All other pi kinds (state_change/telemetry/mulch.record.skipped/reap.*) return null so the ~850 noise events never reach the list. messageDedupeKey keys tool/thinking on event id (stream-only, may legitimately repeat) and user/agent on (kind,content) for transcript<->stream dedupe. Chat.tsx Bubble branches to ToolRow (muted monospace, destructive on error) and ThinkingRow (collapsed details). ConversationDetail header surfaces anchoringRun.data.state via StateBadge so the surface never looks dead.

content: warren-b265 (pl-0008 step 3): plotsApi.startBrainstorm (POST /brainstorm), runsApi.createInteractive (mode=interactive sugar), and plotsApi.formalize (POST /plots/:id/formalize) plus their types (StartBrainstormInput/Response, SuggestedIntent, FormalizePlotResponse) are gone from src/ui/src/api/{client,types}.ts. Their only consumers were the retired mode=interactive panels, so deleting src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx and the StartBrainstormDialog in Plots.tsx (warren-af60's scope) was required to keep tsc green — the two issues are mutually coupled.

warren-af60 (pl-0008 step 4): the dead interactive-panel.tsx UI panel + Plots.tsx StartBrainstormDialog were already deleted by the coupled warren-b265 API-client sweep (commit b74a11ab) to keep tsc green ('mutually coupled'). The residual scope for af60 was stale-comment hygiene only: drop dangling references to the deleted interactive-panel.tsx in plot-detail/helpers.ts, plot-detail/run-plan.tsx, and PlotDetail.tsx header comments, plus the formalize-summary non-goal line in components/Chat.tsx and the 'Start brainstorming' affordance note in components/DefaultLanding.tsx. No UI tests referenced the removed panels (only 2 .test files exist under src/ui).

WorkspacePage (src/ui/src/pages/Workspace.tsx, warren-dc54 / pl-0008 step 5) is the /workspace route: one row per Plot (durable spine) merging the old Plots + Leveret lists. Joins plotsApi.list (server ?status / ?filter=needs_attention chip contract preserved) with conversationsApi.list({}) resolved client-side to the most-recent active conversation per plotId. Columns: name, project, status+needs-you reasons, intent preview, active-conversation indicator (links to /workspace/:id?tab=shape), last activity. Actions: NewConversationButton + NewPlotButton. The shared New Plot dialog lives in src/ui/src/components/NewPlotButton.tsx (NewPlotButton + NewPlotDialog, destination prop defaults /plots) — extracted from Plots.tsx so both /plots and /workspace drive the same create flow.

WorkspaceDetailPage (src/ui/src/pages/WorkspaceDetail.tsx) is the /workspace/:id route — keyed by plotId, loads via plotsApi.get with 5s refetch, reuses PlotDetail's 404/RefreshProjectsCTA recovery branch. Persistent header (PlotNameEditor + StatusTransitionControl + PlotSyncButton + project/summary links) above Shape/Plan/Run/Activity tabs; tab state lives in a ?tab= query param (useSearchParams, default 'shape'). Tab panels are placeholder stubs to be filled by pl-0008 steps 7-10 (warren-3de4/e33f/d17f/ef97). Registered in App.tsx after the /workspace list route.

name: workspace-shape-tab-reuses-conversation-surface
description: WorkspaceDetail Shape tab (warren-3de4 / pl-0008 step 7) reuses the shared ConversationSplitView (src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/conversation-surface.tsx) — extracted from ConversationDetail.tsx so the /leveret/:id page and the Workspace Shape tab render an identical split-view (streamed Chat with sendMessage->POST /conversations/:id/messages + dynamic fieldsFromIntent/patchFromFields intent editor + SendOffButton). ShapeTab resolves the Plot's conversation via conversationsApi.list({plot:plot.id}), preferring status==='active' then the latest row (post-send-off closed -> surface renders read-only/quiet); NewConversationButton affordance shows when none exist. Structural tests in src/ui-tests now read split-view body assertions from conversation-surface.tsx.

content: Plan tab in src/ui/src/pages/WorkspaceDetail.tsx resolves the Plot's conversation via conversationsApi.list({plot}), picks the one carrying plannerRunId (post-send-off it's closed but still stamped), polls runsApi.get(plannerRunId) with the RUN_TERMINAL_STATES refetchInterval pattern, links out to /runs/:plannerRunId for the generated sd plan (no inline renderer), and gates the existing DispatchPlanButton (conversation-detail/dispatch-plan-dialog.tsx) behind a useState sign-off checkbox shown only when planner run state==='succeeded'. Dispatch flows over the unchanged /plan-runs path. Adding ~120 lines of UI tripped the bundle-size ratchet (+3679B js / +476B css) — re-baseline with 'bun run check:bundle-size --update' (within auto-raise cap); a stale dist also makes check:coverage's check-bundle-size.test.ts fail, so re-baseline before re-running coverage.

name: workspace-run-tab-embeds-plan-run-execution
description: Workspace Run tab (warren-d17f / pl-0008 step 9) lives at src/ui/src/pages/workspace-detail/run-tab.tsx. Resolves the dispatched plan-run from the Plot via the PlanRunRow.plotId back-link: planRunsApi.list({project: plot.project_id}) filtered to pr.plotId === plot.id, newest-first. Reuses the shared PlanRunChildTable (extracted from PlanRunDetail into src/ui/src/pages/plan-run-detail/child-table.tsx) for children + per-child PR-merge status + terminal state, and renders PlotAutoDoneNotice mirroring the §11.P coordinator auto-done transition when every child prMergedAt is set / plot.status === 'done'.

name: workspace-detail-tab-modules
description: Workspace detail tabs (pl-0008) live as standalone modules under src/ui/src/pages/workspace-detail/ (run-tab.tsx, activity-tab.tsx) imported into WorkspaceDetail.tsx, keeping the shell under the 500-line size budget. The Activity tab ports PlotDetail's ActivityFeed + SubstratePanel verbatim (both read event_log/paused_runs/attachments off the PlotEnvelope the shell already loads via plotsApi.get) and adds a summary link + closed-conversation history fetched via conversationsApi.list({plot}).

content: Layout.tsx sidebar now renders a single 'Workspace' nav item (Network icon, needs-you badge) gated on anyHasPlot, replacing the former Leveret+Plots pair (WORKSPACE_NAV_ITEM). Legacy routes redirect in App.tsx: /plots and /leveret -> <Navigate to='/workspace'>; /plots/:id -> PlotToWorkspaceRedirect (->/workspace/:id); /leveret/:id -> ConversationToWorkspaceRedirect (fetches conversation, resolves conversation.plotId, then ->/workspace/:plotId?tab=shape, falling back to /workspace on miss). Both redirect components live in src/ui/src/components/WorkspaceRedirects.tsx. DefaultLanding now lands plot-enabled deployments on /workspace. Page shells Leveret.tsx/Plots.tsx/ConversationDetail.tsx/PlotDetail.tsx deleted; their reused sub-component trees (leveret/, conversation-detail/, plot-detail/) kept. /plots/:id/summary (PlotSummaryPage) retained.

README's UI feature bullet and SPEC §11.O.Plot.UI both carry a 'superseded by Workspace collapse (pl-0008)' note rather than rewriting the historical Plot-centric phase-3 record. The §11.O brainstorm/formalize subsection is flagged removed: the mode=interactive path (POST /brainstorm, POST /plots/:id/formalize, brainstorm built-in) was deleted; the Leveret mode=conversation Shape tab replaces it. LEVERET.md no longer exists (removed pre-collapse, commit 234f9c8e). Bundle budgets ratcheted DOWN after the collapse — re-baseline with 'bun run check:bundle-size --update'. docs/http-api.md + docs/openapi.yaml needed no regen (prior steps kept them in sync).

description: buildChatMessages (src/ui/src/components/chat-messages.ts, warren-f4b8/pl-2e59 step 1) previously concatenated all transcript rows ahead of all stream messages, each group sorted by its own seq; transcript seq and event seq are independent numbering systems, so any turn straddling the boundary mis-ordered.
resolution: Merge transcript+stream on a single unified ordering key (wall-clock ts), tie-break transcript-before-stream (source 0/1) so the persisted copy survives dedupe, then dedupe on messageDedupeKey keeping the first chronological occurrence.

name: SortableTableHead primitive
description: Canonical sortable column-header primitive lives in src/ui/src/components/ui/sortable-table-head.tsx (warren-9440 / pl-dfb5 step 1). SortableTableHead<K> renders a TableHead carrying aria-sort plus an inner button with a ChevronUp/ChevronDown that only appears when active; props are columnKey, sort: SortState<K> ({key: K|null, direction: 'asc'|'desc'}), onSort(key), align. Pure helpers (ariaSortFor, nextSortState, SortState/SortDirection types) are split into the sibling sortable-table-head.helpers.ts so they unit-test under the root 'bun test' runner WITHOUT importing react (root runner can't resolve react/jsx-dev-runtime — importing the .tsx in a test throws). The .tsx re-exports the helpers for ergonomic single-import. Runs.tsx is the first adopter (replaced its local SortHeader); it keeps its own 3-state toggleSort cycle and feeds sortState={key,direction}; nextSortState is the default 2-state toggle for other callers. Remaining tables (Workspace, Projects, PlanRuns, Agents) migrate in warren-5562.

name: useClientSort hook for client-side table sorting
description: useClientSort (src/ui/src/hooks/use-client-sort.ts) is the canonical client-side sorting companion to SortableTableHead for tables whose data is already in memory (Projects, PlanRuns, Agents, Workspace). Callers pass an ascending Comparator per column; the hook owns SortState and toggles via nextSortState. Pure applySort + compareStrings live in use-client-sort.helpers.ts (react-free, mirrors sortable-table-head.helpers.ts split) so they're unit-testable under root 'bun test' without installing UI deps. Use NoInfer<K> on the options param so K is inferred from the comparators record, not the initialKey literal (else the strict build-time tsc narrows K to one key). Server-paginated Runs keeps driving sort through its query.

content: Mobile pattern adoption (warren-42ba, pl-dfb5 step 6): list/form/detail pages import from @/components/ui/responsive.ts instead of re-typing ad-hoc strings. responsiveCardHeaderRow replaces 'flex flex-row flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-2 space-y-0' (and the non-wrapping Projects header) on Runs/Projects/CostAnalytics/plot-detail/substrate-panel CardHeaders. responsiveTrailingControl drives the PlanRuns project-filter select (w-full sm:ml-auto sm:w-auto). responsiveFormControl is appended at the END of a select/input className template literal after stripping the inline h-9/text-sm so the 44px/16px phone sizing wins (NewRun/NewPlanRun/Agents/CostAnalytics/RunAnalytics). responsiveFooterActions+responsiveFooterButton stack footer buttons full-width on phones (NewRun/NewPlanRun/RunDetail steer). Tables already scroll via the Table primitive's overflow-auto wrapper, so cells were left as-is.

name: generalized-dispatch-plan-dialog
description: DispatchPlanDialog (src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/dispatch-plan-dialog.tsx, warren-585d/pl-3fc4 step 6) is generalized for use OUTSIDE a conversation: props { projectId, planId?(pre-fill), planIdLocked?, plotId?, onOpenChange }. Dropped the required plannerRunId. Pure logic (computeBindablePlot, computeSubmittable, buildPlanRunInput, readFrontmatter) is extracted to dispatch-plan-dialog.helpers.ts and unit-tested in dispatch-plan-dialog.helpers.test.ts — the UI package has no React testing library, so component logic must be extracted to .helpers.ts for bun:test coverage. Dispatch still goes over planRunsApi.create (POST /plan-runs) unchanged.

content: ReadyPlansView (src/ui/src/pages/ready-plans.tsx, warren-ce62/pl-3fc4 step 7) is the 'Ready to dispatch' tab body on PlanRunsPage. Toggled via PlanRunsTab state ('plan-runs'|'ready'); fetches projectsApi.readyPlans(projectId) with 5s refetch, gated on a selected project (enabled: hasProject) since the endpoint is per-project — prompts to pick a project when none selected. Each row renders a DispatchPlanButton pre-filled+locked (planIdLocked) with the plan id, project, and plotId={hasPlot ? plan.id : null} for one-click dispatch. Structural test in src/ui-tests/ready-plans-tab.test.ts (no React harness).
